Molecular and Cell Biology of Lipids Special Issue: Cholesterol 2000 (Festschrift issue in honor of Konrad Bloch). The text you provided appears to reference a special issue of the journal titled "Cholesterol and Atherosclerosis", published in 2000 as a Festschrift in honor of Konrad Bloch, a pioneering biochemist renowned for his groundbreaking work on cholesterol biosynthesis. Here's a concise summary and context for this issue: --- ### Key Context and Content 1. Konrad Bloch's Legacy Konrad Bloch (1912–2000) was a Nobel laureate (1964) who elucidated the mechanism of cholesterol biosynthesis, a cornerstone of lipid biology. His work laid the foundation for understanding lipid metabolism and its role in diseases like atherosclerosis. 2. Special Issue Focus The 2000 special issue likely includes: - Review articles summarizing advances in cholesterol metabolism and its link to atherosclerosis. - Original research on topics such as: - Lipoprotein biology (LDL, HDL). - Mechanisms of cholesterol transport and cellular uptake. - Genetic and molecular pathways in atherosclerosis. - Therapeutic strategies targeting lipid disorders (e.g., statins). 3. Relevance to Atherosclerosis The issue would emphasize how dysregulated cholesterol homeostasis contributes to plaque formation, inflammation, and cardiovascular disease, reflecting Bloch’s influence on both basic science and clinical research. --- ### How to Access the Issue - Journal Archives: Check academic databases like PubMed, JSTO

texts.full_text: (pre-)release compare equal. .. versionadded:: 1.9.0 Parameters ---------- vstring : str Numpy version string (``np.__version__``). Examples -------- >>> from numpy.lib import NumpyVersion >>> if NumpyVersion(np.__version__) < '1.7.0'): ... print('skip') skip >>> NumpyVersion('1.7') # raises ValueError, add ".0" """ def __init__(self, vstring): self.vstring = vstring ver_main = re.match(r'\d[.]\d+[.]\d+', vstring) if not ver_main: raise ValueError("Not a valid numpy version string") self.version = ver_main.group() self.major, self.minor, self.bugfix = [int(x) for x in self.version.split('.')] if len(vstring) == ver_main.end(): self.pre_release = 'final' else: alpha = re.match(r'a\d', vstring[ver_main.end():]) beta = re.match(r'b\d', vstring[ver_main.end():]) rc = re.match(r'rc\d', vstring[ver_main.end():]) pre_rel = [m for m in [alpha, beta, rc] if m is not None] if pre_rel: self.pre_release = pre_rel[0].group() else: self.pre_release = '' self.is_devversion = bool(re.search(r'.dev', vstring)) def _compare_version(self, other): """Compare major.minor.bugfix""" if self.major == other.major: if self.minor == other.minor: if self.bugfix == other.bugfix: vercmp = 0 elif self.bugfix > other.bugfix: vercmp = 1 else: vercmp = -1 elif self.minor > other.minor: vercmp = 1 else: vercmp = -1 elif self.major > other.major: vercmp = 1 else: vercmp = -1 return vercmp def _compare_pre_release(self, other): """Compare alpha/beta/rc/final.""" if self.pre_release == other.pre_release: vercmp = 0 elif self.pre_release == 'final': vercmp = 1 elif other.pre_release == 'final': vercmp = -1 elif self.pre_release > other.pre_release: vercmp = 1 else: vercmp = -1 return vercmp def _compare(self, other): if not isinstance(other, (basestring, NumpyVersion)): raise ValueError("Invalid object to compare with NumpyVersion.") if isinstance(other, basestring): other = NumpyVersion(other) vercmp = self._compare_version(other) if vercmp == 0: # Same x.y.z version, check for alpha/beta/rc vercmp = self._compare_pre_release(other) if vercmp == 0: # Same version and same pre-release, check if dev version if self.is_devversion is other.is_devversion: vercmp = 0 elif self.is_devversion: vercmp = -1 else: vercmp = 1 return vercmp def

texts.full_text: starch does not cook long enough to absorb enough water, it will not completely gelatinize." -Talbot and Kamozawa, Ideas in Food, page 120 One of the problems with rice-based pastas is they get gummy fast, that is, rice pasta gels and gets sticky when cooked because the cell walls of the starch (mostly amylopectin, with a low but appreciable amount of amylose) burst when cooked. If a starch can undergo retrogradation (a process that is responsible for the staling of bread but also the reinforcement of starch cell walls, a good thing for rice pasta and potatoes), some of this sticky/gummy texture can be remedied. But rice is generally low in amylose, one of the keys in retrogradation. The amylopectin fraction of rice starch forms crystals as it cools that melt as it is heated up. Anyone who has eaten cold rice before reheating it has experienced this. Rice pasta is similarly grainy when cold. Rice pasta, however, is not rice.

texts.full_text: voices of the performers soar into the sky with the birds, with selections from La Boheme, Carmen and even a lively rendition of “Poor Wandering One” from The Pirates of Penzance. Regardless of the hefty serving of culture, I am starving, and it’s a prime opportunity to sample the fare at ‘wichcraft, one of three olive-colored pavilions that serve sandwiches, coffee, pastry and ice cream in Bryant Park (http://www.wichcraftnyc.com/). New York Magazine recently profiled ‘wichcraft as one of its 101 Best Cheap Eats in New York. I join the queue and place an order with one of the orange-clad employees for chicken salad, walnuts, roasted tomatoes, pickled red onions, and frisee on multigrain bread, perhaps the longest list of ingredients and condiments for a single sandwich, ever. I find a spot to stand on the North end of the lawn and munch on my sandwich. I listen as the passion flares on stage and I rapidly develop a tragic love affair with my sandwich which is vanishing quickly. The flavorful chicken takes center stage, the roasted tomatoes add a subtle dramatic note and the frisee a touch of comic relief, all surrounded by the artistry of artisanal multi-grain bread. For the finale, I harmonize with a chocolate cupcake with cream filling, which evokes memories of the classic Hostess Cupcake. It is Devil’s Food Chocolate, to be exact.

texts.full_text: such as reduced traction, increased wear, decreased fuel efficiency, and even blowouts in severe cases. Therefore, monitoring tire pressure is crucial for maintaining optimal performance and safety. A tire pressure monitoring sensor (TPMS) is designed to continuously measure the air pressure within each tire and transmit this information wirelessly to a receiver unit, typically integrated into the vehicle's dashboard or control system. The TPMS consists of several key components: a pressure sensor, temperature sensor, microcontroller, wireless communication module, and a battery. These elements work together to accurately detect and relay real-time tire pressure data to alert drivers when there is an abnormality in any of the tires' pressures. The system can be programmed with specific thresholds for low or high pressure that trigger alerts if exceeded, ensuring timely intervention before potential problems escalate. Installation and maintenance are also straightforward; each TPMS unit is typically mounted inside the tire rim near the valve stem. The sensor remains relatively protected from environmental elements while maintaining proximity to accurate readings of internal pressure fluctuations due to driving conditions or temperature changes. Furthermore, these sensors can be integrated with other vehicle safety systems for a comprehensive approach to monitoring and maintaining overall vehicular health. In summary, tire pressure monitoring sensors are essential tools in modern vehicles that enhance safety by providing continuous feedback on tire air pressure levels. Their application ensures that tires operate within optimal conditions, reducing risks of failure and contributing positively to the performance efficiency and durability of vehicle wheels

The apparatus utilizes a novel technique for packet synchronization and error correction, ensuring that the information is accurately stored and can be subsequently reproduced without loss of data. 3. Detailed Description of the Invention The recording apparatus of the present invention is comprised of several components, including a packet detector, a packet synchronization unit, an error correction unit, and a data recording unit. The packet detector is responsible for identifying the individual packets within the serial datastream of the information signal. The packet synchronization unit then synchronizes the packets, ensuring that they are correctly positioned within the corresponding tracks on the record carrier. texts.full_text: und Mutations in parkin and PTEN-induced kinase 1 (Pink1) lead to autosomal recessive forms of Parkinson's disease (PD). parkin and Pink1 encode a ubiquitin-protein ligase and a mitochondrially localized serine/threonine kinase, respectively. Recent studies have implicated Parkin and Pink1 in a common and evolutionarily conserved pathway for protecting mitochondrial integrity. Results To systematically identify novel components of the PD pathways, we generated a genetic background that allowed us to perform a genome-wide F1 screen for modifiers of Drosophila parkin (park) and Pink1 mutant phenotype. From screening ~80% of the fly genome, we identified a number of cytological regions that interact with park and/or Pink1. Among them, four cytological regions were selected for identifying corresponding PD-interacting genes. By analyzing smaller deficiency chromosomes, available transgenic RNAi lines, and P-element insertions, we identified five PD-interacting genes. Among them, opa1 and drp1 have been previously implicated in mitochondrial dynamics and are considered to play important roles in mitochondrial fusion and fission, respectively. Opa1, or optic atrophy 1, is a dynamin-related GTPase that is important for mitochondrial outer membrane fusion and cristae organization, and its dysfunction is correlated with optic nerve. Drp1, or dynamin-related gene 1, is a GTPase that promotes mitochondrial fission, and its dysregulation is attributed to many neurodegenerative conditions, including PD. The identification of opa1 and drp1 as PD-interacting mutations in this molecular screen demonstrates the significance of mitochondrial interactions in the pathogenesis of PD, and it aligns with the known roles of Parkin and Pink1 in mitochondrial quality control, including mitophagy (the selective destruction of damaged mitochondria). The other PD-interacting genes identified in the screen, while not previously linked to mitochondrial dynamics, can therefore play important roles in mitochondrial function, stress response, and mitochondrial survival, thereby creating new targets for clinical intervention in PD. texts.full_text: the guild board this past spring, but he’s no stranger as he served as president from 1984-1996 and on the board several times over the years. He retired last year after nearly four decades with The Post-Gazette. History The Newspaper Guild of Pittsburgh is an affiliate chapter of the Communications Workers of America (CWA), a union representing more than half-a-million workers in the United States, Canada and Puerto Rico. CWA has been called “the most progressive union in America.” Our guild was chartered on July 17, 1934 by local journalists who had joined up with other newsroom employees from around the country to form The Newspaper Guild — a national union that today represents more than 20,000 newspaper and digital media workers. We are Local 38061 of CWA District Four which is based in Cleveland. Our district covers Pennsylvania, West Virginia, Ohio, Michigan, Indiana and Kentucky. The Pittsburgh Post-Gazette was one of the first newspapers to unionize when its newsroom employees voted on Dec. 29, 1934 to join The Newspaper Guild — only a few months after our local had been chartered. At that time, it became the third largest guild in CWA District Four with nearly 50 members. Our sister paper at the Pittsburgh Press also joined the union soon afterward and remained a member for more than six decades until its last edition was published on June 13, 1987 — when the owners of both papers closed it down to save money by consolidating operations. We still mourn that loss. But our local survived and thrived in spite of all the changes in the newspaper business since then: from the closing of the Pittsburgh Press, to the rise of digital journalism and the decline in advertising revenues for newspapers nationwide as they have struggled with how to compete on a level playing field with Google, Facebook and other social media giants. texts.full_text: (TNF-alpha), interleukin-1 (IL-1), and interleukin-6 (IL-6), is upregulated, contributing to the inflammatory response and tissue damage. Additionally, the activation of trypsinogen and the subsequent activation of other proteases, such as chymotrypsin and elastase, play a crucial role in the autodigestion of the pancreas, causing further tissue damage and inflammation. The role of oxidative stress in the pathogenesis of acute pancreatitis has been increasingly recognized. Reactive oxygen species (ROS) and reactive nitrogen species (RNS) are generated during pancreatitis, causing oxidative damage to cellular components, including lipids, proteins, and DNA. Antioxidant defense mechanisms, including superoxide dismutase (SOD), catalase (CAT), and glutathione peroxidase (GPx), are upregulated in response to oxidative stress. However, when antioxidant defenses are overwhelmed, oxidative stress can lead to cell death, exacerbating pancreatic inflammation and worsening the clinical outcome. One of the challenges in the management of acute pancreatitis is the identification of patients at high risk for severe disease and complications, such as pancreatic necrosis, infection, and leakage of pancreatic enzymes. Several scoring systems, including the Ranson, Imrie, and revised Atlanta criteria, have been developed to predict the severity of acute pancreatitis. These scoring systems take into account clinical, laboratory, and radiographic parameters, such as the presence of local or systemic complications, the degree of pancreatic edema, and the extent of inflammatory response. However, none of these scoring systems have proven to be perfect, and there remains a need for more accurate and reliable biomarkers to guide clinical decision-making. In recent years, much attention has been focused on the potential role of pro- and anti-inflammatory cytokines, as well as non-coding RNAs, such as microRNAs, in the pathogenesis and prognosis of acute pancreatitis. texts.full_text: female usually swims away from the male, who will chase her until she is ready for mating and then allows him to mount on top of her back. After successful copulation, she begins searching for suitable nesting sites along shorelines. After mating occurs, eggs are laid 3–4 weeks later during late April through July (earlier in southern parts). Females lay clutches of four or more oblong-shaped eggs that measure about one inch long and weigh between six to ten grams each. Eggs hatch within three months; incubation periods depend on temperature and can vary from sixty-one to one hundred four days for certain nests. Age at sexual maturity is estimated as being five years old, but some researchers believe it may take up eight years or more before they reach full size (males mature sooner than females). Lifespan averages around 20-35 years in captivity; wild populations have been known to live over forty years! Distribution Diamondback terrapins are found along both coasts of North America from New Jersey southward throughout Florida and into Mexico. They also occur on islands off the southeastern U.S., such as Cape Hatteras National Seashore. In addition, they can be found in parts of Bermuda; however, there have been no recent sightings within that country since 1967 due to habitat loss from human activities like development or dredging

texts.full_text: ok over the villages across Laguna from Manila to Bulacan. Chinese rebels descended to Tanay and established camps in Monte Tan-ay and Pantay. A popular story recounts how a Franciscan priest, Fr. Geronimo de Frías, hid the image of La Purísima inside the jungle nearby. It was said that three Chinese soldiers unwittingly discovered the image and tried to destroy it with their spears. The spears are miraculously thrown back to the throwers. Driven by fear, the remaining Chinese who witnessed the event committed suicide by hanging himself in the tree in a place now known as Pinagbigtihan (Place of Hanging) to the present day. The town suffered a heavy blow during the uprising which only ended when a combined Filipino-Spanish forces attacked their camps on 1640. After the rebels were oppressed, the priests transferred the parish to its present site, formally establishing the town of Tanay in the same year. In 1678, Fr. Pedro de Espallargas initiated the building of a stone church and the first church was completed in 1680 through the contributions of churchgoers and devotees of Purísima Concepción. The first mass was celebrated on April 20, 1680. However, the structure did not last long because of natural calamities. By 1773, the construction of the present church began through the initiative of Fr. Alonso de Fentanes

texts.full_text: Such heat checking is known to be the cause of thermal shock or thermal fatigue related crack formation, crack propagation, and ultimately catastrophic failure. The problem of heat checking is attributed to the relatively poor thermal properties of the cobalt (the binder material) when compared to that of the tungsten carbide. Prior attempts to correct this problem, to increase the heat checking resistance of WC—Co, has been to reduce the cobalt binder content and balance other mechanical properties of the composition through grain size adjustment. However, reducing the cobalt binder content adversely impacts other properties of the resulting cemented tungsten carbide material. Generally speaking, as you decrease the cobalt binder content you also reduce the fracture toughness, and increase the hardness, Of the cemented tungsten carbide, thereby making the composition brittle and more susceptible to fracture and failure. As you increase hardness you also increase wear resistance, but this is all at the expense of fracture toughness. Fracture toughness is a limiting factor in demanding industrial applications such as high penetration drilling, where WC—Co inserts often exhibit gross brittle fracture that leads to catastrophic failure. Thus, prior attempts at addressing unwanted heat checking, to reduce or control thermal shock related catastrophic failure, has been at the expense of reduced fracture toughness, which also is known to cause catastrophic failure. It is, therefore, desirable that a cermet composition be developed that has improved thermal shock resistance when compared to conventional cemented tungsten carbide materials. It is desirable that such cermet composition display improved thermal shock resistance without sacrificing such properties as fracture toughness and wear resistance when compared to conventional cemented tungsten carbide materials. It is desired that cermet compositions of this invention be adapted for use in such applications as rock bits, hammer bits, mini

texts.full_text: goal of simplifying my life by organizing my home and doing things to make cleaning quicker and easier. I began by purging closets and drawers. I pulled everything out and in very little time separated clothing, electronics, and toys into three categories: keep, trash, and donate. BUT I am still left with papers I have not gone through to determine what to keep and what to trash. Pa pers aside, there have been other accomplishments toward meeting my goal. I have made specific spaces for storing materials for all my activities: writing, sewing, music, jewelry, painting. Glass top furniture pieces have been eliminated or replaced with easier to keep clean pieces. The glass and wood coffee table is now one of those all wood lift top tables with storage. Nick-Nacks that require tedious dusting have been removed. Even the outside of my home is being simplified. No more painting and staining the deck and porches. Wood has been replaced with composite decking. Purge…purge…purge. Once I have the house done, it’s on to the storage shed. Now, back to those papers.
